seo.title: LlamaIndex Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for LlamaIndex agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using LlamaIndex face heightened risks when indexing sensitive public records or policy documents, where retrieval errors or hallucinations can lead to misinformed decisions affecting citizens. Compliance with FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 demands verifiable AI outputs to prevent national security breaches and ensure explainability. Vex provides runtime guardrails to verify LlamaIndex query responses against these strict standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and auditing of cloud-based AI systems processing federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs every query_engine.query() call with verifiable traces, enabling FedRAMP-compliant audit trails for retrieval relevance and response accuracy.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates controls for AI risk management, including verification of outputs against authoritative sources.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies retrieved context relevance and flags hallucinations beyond indexed documents per NIST RA-5 implementation.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainable AI for decisions affecting rights.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides structured verification results showing exact source-document alignment for every response.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI outputs are accessible and usable by citizens with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags incomplete or hallucinated responses that could create accessibility barriers in public-facing systems.

industryRisks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
industryRisks.description: RAG systems return outdated policy docs or unrelated records for citizen inquiries.
industryRisks.example: Wrong retrieval cites repealed regulation, leading to unlawful benefits denial.

industryRisks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
industryRisks.description: LLM fabricates policy interpretations not present in indexed regulations.
industryRisks.example: Fabricated eligibility criteria denies legitimate veteran benefits.

industryRisks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
industryRisks.description: Unrefreshed indexes serve expired laws or procedures.
industryRisks.example: Outdated COVID policy served during active health emergency.

industryRisks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
industryRisks.description: Document splitting cuts off key regulatory clauses mid-sentence.
industryRisks.example: Partial FOIA exemption text creates compliance violation.

implementation.title: Wrap LlamaIndex Query Engine with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect government RAG queries by verifying response fidelity to indexed sources.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader
from vex import verify

# Load government policy documents
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('gov_policies/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

# Vex-wrapped government query
def safe_gov_query(question):
    response = query_engine.query(question)
    verified = verify(
        response.response,
        context=response.source_nodes,
        policy='gov-rag-strict',
        metadata={'classification': 'unclassified'}
    )
    return verified

result = safe_gov_query('What are current FOIA exemptions?')

implementation.title: Index Freshness Monitoring for FISMA
implementation.description: Detect stale indexes serving outdated federal regulations.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage
from vex import verify
import datetime

def verify_index_freshness(index_path: str, max_age_days: int = 7):
    storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir=index_path)
    index = load_index_from_storage(storage_context)
    
    # Check index metadata timestamp
    last_updated = index.index_struct.last_updated
    if (datetime.datetime.now() - last_updated).days > max_age_days:
        raise ValueError('FISMA violation: Stale index')
    
    # Verify with Vex
    test_query = 'Current status of Executive Order 14110?'
    response = index.as_query_engine().query(test_query)
    
    verified = verify(
        response.response,
        context=response.source_nodes,
        policy='index-freshness',
        metadata={'compliance': 'FISMA', 'max_age_days': max_age_days}
    )
    
    return verified

# FISMA compliance check
verify_index_freshness('gov_index_storage/')

caseStudyScenario.heading: LlamaIndex in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
The Department of Veterans Affairs deployed LlamaIndex to index 50,000+ pages of benefits regulations, enabling VA counselors to quickly retrieve eligibility criteria for 10 million veterans. The VectorStoreIndex processed directives from Title 38 CFR, Privacy Act notices, and FOIA implementation guidance. Public-facing chat interfaces answered 5,000 daily queries about healthcare enrollment, disability compensation, and education benefits.

Without verification, the system began serving incorrect responses. Counselors received hallucinated income limits for dependents, irrelevant Privacy Act clauses for FOIA requests, and stale COVID-era policies. A congressional inquiry revealed the system cited a repealed 2022 regulation, wrongfully denying benefits to 247 veterans. The VA faced FISMA audit failures and Section 508 complaints about inaccessible explanations.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails wrapped every query_engine.query() call with 'gov-rag-strict' policies. Retrieval irrelevance dropped 98%, with Vex blocking 1,247 hallucinated responses citing non-existent regulations. Index freshness checks enforced daily rebuilds per FISMA RA-5. Explainable verification traces satisfied Executive Order 14110 requirements, enabling appeal processes. The VA achieved FedRAMP Moderate authorization and restored public trust, processing 99.7% compliant responses.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your LlamaIndex deployment today to achieve FedRAMP and FISMA compliance.
